risk_if_not_implemented: Without Heuristic / Nonsignature-Based Detection, security events may go undetected, allowing threats to persist unnoticed and increasing breach dwell time.

compensating_control_1.name: Software & Firmware Patching
compensating_control_1.description: Mechanisms exist to conduct software patching for all deployed Technology Assets, Applications and/or Services (TAAS), including firmware.
compensating_control_1.justification: Software & Firmware Patching (VPM-05) provides vulnerability management that compensates for the absence of Heuristic / Nonsignature-Based Detection (END-04.4) by reducing the exploitable attack surface by addressing known weaknesses before they are leveraged. Given the technology-focused nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.

compensating_control_2.name: Malicious Code Protection (Anti-Malware)
compensating_control_2.description: Mechanisms exist to utilize antimalware technologies to detect and eradicate malicious code.
compensating_control_2.justification: Malicious Code Protection (Anti-Malware) (END-04) provides overlapping security capability that compensates for the absence of Heuristic / Nonsignature-Based Detection (END-04.4) by addressing related risk objectives through an alternative control mechanism aligned with Technology applicability. Given the technology-focused nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.
